signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Fed Hawkishness
signals.text: USD outperforms as the G8 leader (+0.648%) while AUD lags (-1.188%), creating a 1.837% spread. The divergence is driven by Philadelphia Fed President Paulson's endorsement of current restrictive policy and market hike pricing, alongside a bear steepener in the US yield curve (2s10s +59.7bp). Despite a -60bp carry deficit vs AUD (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), USD strength dominates on safe-haven demand and surging US 10Y yields at 4.667%. AUD/USD at 0.7102 remains under pressure as the G8 laggard.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading the RBNZ) vs a market hike bias for the 27 May meeting. This institutional short positioning diverges from extreme retail sentiment (86% long), creating a high-conviction contrarian setup. If RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance, the crowded spec short is vulnerable to a squeeze, while retail longs face capitulation risk if price breaks below 0.5800. NZD/USD at 0.5828.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol + ECB Hawkishness
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1605 testing recent lows as ECB's Nagel and Kocher signal a June hike is 'unavoidable' due to Hormuz Strait energy shocks. Despite hawkish CB rhetoric, EUR COT LF net remains short -8,151. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 5.0% (VRP +2.1%), while 25d RR at -0.53 confirms a persistent put skew. The +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) continues to overpower ECB hike bets.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + BoJ Taper Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.91 remains below the 160.00 intervention threshold, but options markets are pricing heavy downside protection with a strong 25d RR of -1.52 (Saxo). This extreme put skew contradicts the +280bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.53% vs TONA 0.73%), signaling institutional fear of a BoJ policy shift. Analysts suggest BoJ may slow bond tapering in June, which would catalyze a JPY bid. Watch for a break of 160.00 to trigger verbal or physical intervention.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 20.4% vs HV30 at 9.3% generates a massive +11.1% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), the highest in the G10 space. This extreme premium indicates the options market is pricing in significant tail risk or a regime shift that has not yet materialized in realized price action. With AUD as the G8 laggard (-1.188% 1W avg), the high cost of protection suggests a lack of conviction in the current 0.7102 floor. Watch for IV mean-reversion or a volatility-led break lower.
